name: Khatta Moong (Soured Lentils)
recipeInstructions:
1. Let the yogurt sit for 24 hours at room temperature to develop a slightly sour taste.
2. Wash the mung dal thoroughly. In a saucepan, combine the dal, 3 cups water, turmeric, and salt. Bring to a boil, then lower heat, cover loosely, and simmer until the lentils have split open but are still whole (do not let them become mushy). Drain and set aside.
3. Whisk together the yogurt, 3 3/4 cups water, and gram flour in a bowl until a smooth consistency is reached.
4. Grind two of the green chilies and ginger to a paste.
5. Heat ghee in a large saucepan. Add the remaining chilies (halved), cinnamon stick, cumin seeds, and hing. Sizzle for 5-6 seconds.
6. Stir in the yogurt mixture, turmeric, salt, sugar, and chili-ginger paste. Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened for 5-7 minutes.
7. Add the drained lentils and cook an additional 5 minutes until thickened. Garnish with chopped cilantro leaves before serving.
